sentence_reviews.text: State, stability, observability, feedback, and optimal control govern systems that evolve and act over time.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 62-71 and 96-111; lyapunov.html lines 30-43 and 49-51; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76; AIMA contents.html Chapter 14 'States and observations' / 'Transition and sensor models' lines 361-365 and Chapter 26.5 'Planning and Control' / 'Optimal control' lines 704-716.
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support state-based dynamical systems, stability analysis, feedback, and optimal control. Observability is only indirectly supported by AIMA's states/observations and transition/sensor-model topics, so the combined sentence is a defensible synthesis rather than exact source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Dynamical systems & control theory is to connect Differential equations, Linear algebra to downstream capabilities including Neuroscience & cognitive science, Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states, Multimodal, simulated & streaming data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 62-71 and 91-111; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76; AIMA contents.html Chapter 1.2.4 'Neuroscience' and 1.2.7 'Control theory and cybernetics' lines 26-34; Chapter 14 temporal models lines 361-381; Chapter 22 reinforcement learning lines 598-619; Chapter 26 robotics lines 693-719.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support differential equations and linear algebra as machinery in control, and AIMA covers neuroscience, temporal probabilistic models, reinforcement learning, and robotics. The exact bridge to POMDPs and multimodal/simulated/streaming data is graph-derived and not directly stated by the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html Chapter 1.2.4 'Neuroscience' and Chapter 1.2.7 'Control theory and cybernetics' lines 26-34.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA places both neuroscience and control theory among AI foundations, but the source does not state that dynamical systems and control theory are a substrate for neuroscience and cognitive science.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html Chapter 14 'Probabilistic Reasoning over Time' including states, observations, transition and sensor models, hidden Markov models, and dynamic Bayesian networks lines 361-381; Chapter 22 reinforcement learning and policy search lines 598-619.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA directly supports temporal state/transition models and reinforcement-learning policy topics that overlap with Markov and MDP-style formalisms. The phrase 'substrate for' and the explicit POMDP/belief-state grouping are atlas synthesis rather than exact source language.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 14-17, 35-36, and 86-90; AIMA contents.html Chapter 25 computer vision lines 662-690 and Chapter 26 robotics lines 693-719.
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence supports simulation and robotics/perception contexts for control, but it does not directly establish the broad multimodal/streaming-data substrate claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Differential equations, Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 67-75 and 109-111; lyapunov.html lines 35-51; AIMA contents.html Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' lines 768-774 and Chapter 26.5 control topics lines 704-716.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support explicit mathematical conditions, state/control differential equations, and linear algebraic machinery, but they do not directly state the empirical-mismatch caution. The dependency clause is defensible but omits the packet's optimization dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Differential equations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 67-71 and 98-110; lyapunov.html lines 35-51; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources repeatedly formulate control and stability problems as differential equations. The dependency is well supported, though 'rests on' is still an atlas formulation.

sentence_reviews.text: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 67-71 and 91-100; lqr.html lines 21-25, 33-47, and 57-58; AIMA contents.html Appendix A.2 'Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Algebra' lines 768-774.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources use state vectors, matrices, rank, inverses, quadratic forms, and LQR matrix equations in control. This supports the dependency, although the exact rationale is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: Dynamical systems & control theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Underactuated Robotics intro.html lines 16-17, 62-64, and 111-112; lqr.html lines 43-58 and 69-76; AIMA contents.html Chapter 26.5.2 'Trajectory optimization for kinematic planning' and 26.5.4 'Optimal control' lines 704-716.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support optimization as an important and common part of modern control, especially trajectory optimization, LQR, and optimal control. They do not support the stronger claim that the whole field rests on optimization theory.

edge_reviews.notes: The edge direction is correct under the atlas convention: control/dynamical systems use differential equations as a prerequisite modeling language. Core strength is defensible because the inspected control notes formulate central state, stability, feedback, and optimal-control material with differential equations.
The inspected evidence supports state vectors, matrices, rank/full-row-rank conditions, inverses, quadratic forms, and LQR matrix equations as central control machinery. Core strength is defensible.

The evidence supports optimization as a common ingredient in trajectory optimization, LQR, and optimal control. It does not support 'requires' as the narrowest relationship for all dynamical systems and control theory; 'uses' or another non-prerequisite relation would be narrower. The common strength label is defensible.
